articles.title: Ship catches fire off Oman hours after US strike; British crew escapes on lifeboat
articles.summary: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers.

articles.title: No change: Conductor tells Karnataka transport minister to get off bus
articles.summary: Transport minister Byrathi Suresh experienced commuter problems firsthand. A bus conductor asked him to exit for lacking exact change.

articles.title: 9 dead in Pune's Moshi building collapse; waste plant operator chief blames heavy rainfall
articles.summary: Seven more bodies were recovered from the collapsed building at the Moshi waste-to-energy plant. This recovery brought the total death toll from the incident to eight people.

articles.title: FIFA World Cup: England in semis as Bellingham’s double sinks Norway amid VAR fury
articles.summary: At TOI Sports Desk, reporters work around the clock to bring you comprehensive updates from the world of sports. Expect nuanced match reports, previews, and reviews, along with statistics-based technical analysis, the latest social media trends, and expert insights across cricket, football, tennis, badminton, hockey, motorsports, wrestling, boxing, shooting, athletics, and more.

articles.title: Canada gives share of bridge profits to US to get Trump nod
articles.summary: The Gordie Howe International Bridge will open on July 27 connecting Detroit and Windsor. Canada agreed to share net toll profits with the United States after construction costs.

articles.title: Bangladesh bars media from publishing Hasina statements
articles.summary: Bangladesh's government has ordered all media to stop broadcasting Sheikh Hasina's statements. This directive follows a court ban on publishing content from the former prime minister.

articles.title: Russians urged to work from home amid fuel crisis
articles.summary: Russia's Novosibirsk region is urging remote work and reduced car travel. This comes amid a deepening fuel crisis affecting many Russian areas.

articles.title: DU UG Admissions 2026: Over 1.9 lakh students register as university gears up for first seat allotment on July 16
articles.summary: Delhi University has recorded over 1.9 lakh registrations for undergraduate admissions through the Common Seat Allocation System (CSAS), with more than 1.64 lakh candidates completing preference submission. The first seat allotment list will be released on July 16, while a one-time correction window and a new virtual wallet system aim to streamline the admission and fee payment process..

articles.title: AIBE 21 result 2026 likely to be released soon at allindiabarexamination.com: Check steps to download scorecards here
articles.summary: The Bar Council of India is expected to declare the AIBE XXI Result 2026 soon on the official website. Candidates will be able to check and download their scorecards using their roll number and date of birth.

articles.title: NMC approves 50 more MBBS seats at GMC Srinagar, intake rises to 250 from 2026-27
articles.summary: Government Medical College Srinagar will add fifty new MBBS seats. This significant increase will take the annual intake to two hundred fifty students.

articles.title: DU PG admissions 2026: Round 3 minimum allocation scores released at admission.uod.ac.in; check PDF here
articles.summary: The University of Delhi has released Postgraduate Round 3 minimum allocation scores for the 2026-27 academic session. Candidates can check these scores on the official DU admission portal for specific courses and colleges.
